steps.title: Pull out the greatest common factor
Match against the choices
steps.content:
Any factor of the original expression must appear in $p^{17}(7p-11)(7p+11)$.
  • $7p + 11$ — appears directly. Factor.
  • $49p^2 + 121$ — this is a sum of squares, not a difference; it cannot be split out of $49p^2 - 121$. Not a factor.
  • $11p - 7$ — the linear factors are $7p \pm 11$, not $11p \pm 7$. Not a factor.
  • $-72p^{40}$ — the largest power of $p$ in the expression is $p^{19}$, so $p^{40}$ cannot divide it. Not a factor.
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
